title_en: A rare Chinese missile test unnerves Asian neighbours
title_zh: 中国一次罕见的导弹试验令亚洲邻国坐立不安
rubric_en: China moves a step closer to having an operational “nuclear triad”
rubric_zh: 中国正在更接近具备可实战的“核三位一体”能力

paragraphs.en: Secrecy is essential to the operation of submarines that bear nuclear missiles. The vessels, known as boomers, are designed to stay hidden at sea for long periods, poised to fire nuclear-armed ballistic missiles at potential foes. China, however, is especially opaque about its boomers. Although the Pentagon estimates that they have patrolled at sea for a decade, China’s government has never confirmed that. So its announcement on July 6th that one of them had carried out a rare test of an intercontinental ballistic missile in the Pacific Ocean surprised and unnerved the region.
paragraphs.zh: 对携带核导弹的潜艇而言，保密性是其运作的核心要素。这些被称为“弹道导弹核潜艇（boomers）”的舰艇被设计为在海上长期隐蔽待命，以便对潜在敌手发射核动力的弹道导弹。然而，中国在这一领域尤其透明度很低。尽管五角大楼估计它们已经在海上巡航了十年，但中国政府从未确认过这一点。因此，当其在7月6日宣布其中一艘艇在太平洋实施了一次罕见的洲际弹道导弹试验时，令该地区感到意外与不安。

paragraphs.en: The missile carrying a dummy warhead was fired towards international waters, said Xinhua, an official news agency, without saying where it came down. China has test-fired missiles from submarines before but usually closer to its own shores. It has tested long-range ballistic missiles over the Pacific twice before, in 1980 and 2024. Both were launched from land.
paragraphs.zh: 新华社报道称，携带一枚假弹头的导弹被发射向国际水域，但并未披露其落点。中国此前也曾从潜艇试射过导弹，但通常是在更靠近本土海岸的海域。它此前在太平洋上空发射远程弹道导弹的情况有过两次，分别是1980年和2024年，而且两次都来自陆基发射。

paragraphs.en: The test shows that China is “moving closer to a credible triad”, the capacity to launch long-range ballistic missiles from land, sea and air, wrote Frank Rose, a former senior official at the us National Nuclear Security Administration. Wang Qiang, a former professor at China’s National Defence University, predicted on his Weibo social-media account that by next year China would demonstrate the triad’s completion by test-firing an air-launched ballistic missile.
paragraphs.zh: 美国前国家核安全管理局（NNSA）高官弗兰克·罗斯（Frank Rose）写道，这次试验表明中国“正在更接近一个可信的核三位一体”，即从陆基、海基和空基发射远程弹道导弹的能力。中国国防大学前教授王强在微博上预测，到明年中国将通过试射空射弹道导弹，显示其核三位一体的建成。

paragraphs.en: Foreign analysts say China is likely to carry out such tests regularly in the coming years as a way to validate the equipment involved and demonstrate resolve to America. “The United States and its allies must modernise their own deterrent capabilities and…find ways to bring arms control back into play as a tool for constraining China’s build-up,” wrote Mr Rose. China suspended nuclear-arms talks with America in 2024 in protest over arms sales to Taiwan.
paragraphs.zh: 外国分析人士认为，中国可能在未来几年内定期实施此类试验，以验证相关装备并向美国展示决心。“美国及其盟友必须推进自身威慑力量的现代化，并想办法将军备控制重新纳入框架，作为遏制中国军事扩张的一种工具，”罗斯先生写道。中国于2024年暂停了与美国的核军控谈判，以抗议对台军售。

paragraphs.en: The Pentagon has warned that China is expanding its nuclear arsenal and could have more than 1,000 operational warheads by 2030, compared with “the low 600s” in 2024. It is also building quieter submarines. American officials fear this is in preparation for a potential war over Taiwan that could escalate into nuclear confrontation between China and America.
paragraphs.zh: 五角大楼警告称，中国正在扩充其核武库，到2030年可能拥有超过1000枚可用作战核弹头，而2024年仅为“六百多枚”。它还在建造更安静的核潜艇。美国官员担心，这是在为可能发生的台海战争作准备，而该战争可能升级为中国与美国之间的核对峙。

paragraphs.en: The missile, which appeared to have been launched from the South China Sea and to have splashed down in the South Pacific, was likely to be a jl-2 or jl-3 (pictured), said several experts. The former has a range of about 7,200km, meaning it would have to be fired from the mid-Pacific Ocean to hit the western half of the continental United States. The jl-3 has a range of about 10,000km, allowing it to hit parts of the American mainland from Chinese coastal waters. It was on display for the first time at a military parade in Beijing last year.
paragraphs.zh: 这枚导弹似乎是从南海发射并在南太平洋溅落，几位专家认为它很可能是JL-2或JL-3（见图）。前者射程约7200公里，意味着要打击美国本土西部地区，发射点必须在中太平洋海域；JL-3射程约10000公里，可从中国沿海水域击中美国本土的部分地区。它在去年北京的阅兵式上首次亮相。

paragraphs.en: China did notify some governments in the region before the latest test. Still, several voiced concern about the operation, which came on the eve of a nato summit in Ankara and in the midst of American-led joint naval exercises around Hawaii. America’s State Department said China’s “rapid and opaque nuclear-weapons build-up is of great concern”. It urged China to commit to routine notification of such launches, as other nuclear powers have. Australia’s foreign minister, Penny Wong, described the test as “destabilising”.
paragraphs.zh: 中国在最新试验前确实向本地区一些政府通报了此事，但仍有多国表达了担忧。该试验恰逢安卡拉举行北约峰会前夕，并且发生在美国主导的夏威夷周边联合海上演习期间。美国国务院表示，中国“快速且不透明的核武器扩军”令人极为关切，并敦促中国像其他核大国一样，对此类发射进行例行通报。澳大利亚外长黄珀尼（Penny Wong）将这次试验形容为“破坏地区稳定”。

paragraphs.en: China is not alone in test-firing missiles from its boomers. The four other permanent members of the un Security Council—America, Britain, France and Russia—do so regularly. But, given China’s expanding nuclear arsenal and military pressure on Taiwan, this test will heighten regional fears about Chinese intentions and America’s capacity to protect its allies. ■
paragraphs.zh: 中国并非唯一从“弹道导弹核潜艇”（“战略核巡航潜艇”，又称“弹道导弹核潜艇”）进行试射的国家。联合国安理会其他四个常任理事国——美国、英国、法国和俄罗斯——都在定期这样做。不过，考虑到中国核武库的扩张以及对台湾的军事压力，这次试验仍会加剧地区对中国意图以及美国保卫其盟友能力的担忧。■

paragraphs.en: Subscribers can sign up to Drum Tower, our new weekly newsletter, to understand what the world makes of China—and what China makes of the world.
paragraphs.zh: 订阅者可注册 Drum Tower（我们最新的每周通讯），从中了解世界如何看待中国，以及中国如何看待世界。
